parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 6:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Recent from Airtable

### Read Recent from Airtable

airtable — `search` operation against the connected account.

Next: Newest First

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Trim to Digest Size

### Trim to Digest Size

Caps the stream at 15 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Content (AI)

### Summarize Content (AI)

Map-reduce summarization chain — handles long content without blowing the context window.

Next: Render as HTML

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Summarize Content (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Render as HTML

Converts markdown to HTML for rich rendering in email/CMS.

Next: Post Digest to Telegram

### Post Digest to Telegram

telegram — `action` operation against the connected account.

Next: Archive Digest in Airtable

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Archive Digest in Airtable

airtable — `create` operation against the connected account.

Next: Send Summary to Telegram

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Send Summary to Telegram

telegram — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

# Deliver a weekly summary with Airtable and Telegram and log the results

**Pattern:** Scheduled Digest · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Deliver a weekly summary with Airtable and Telegram and log the results. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual exports is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Read Recent from Airtable → Newest First → Trim to Digest Size → Summarize Content (AI) → Render as HTML → Post Digest to Telegram → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to scheduled digest without manual effort
- Anyone automating exports in data
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Responds in seconds
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Groq API key; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your exports process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
